| Host CRM Supabase worker units | `popcrm-contact-sync.service`, `popcrm-summarize.service`, and `popcrm-apply-ignore-rules.service` run `pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` with secrets in mode-600 `/home/ai/.crm-worker.env` | CRM data moved from Directus collections to the shared Supabase backend; the host timers still own Outlook/contact maintenance jobs | If the host is rebuilt, install the unit files from `pm-system/systemd/`, restore `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, run `npm install`, and verify `systemctl cat popcrm-contact-sync.service` points to `crm-worker-supabase.mjs` |

## 7. Task-to-file navigation

| Task | Edit | Do NOT touch |

| Change the data model spec | `docs/data-model.md` | — |

### CRM Supabase worker records unknown domains as triage rows

What changed:
On 2026-06-26, the host-side CRM worker moved from the old Directus collection
contract to `pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` for contact sync, summaries, and
ignore-rule application. It uses the shared Supabase service-role key from
`/home/ai/.crm-worker.env`; Node 20 requires the `ws` package to be passed as
the Supabase realtime transport.

Why:
POP CRM Accounts Triage now reads `crm.ingested_domain` through
`api.crm_ingested_domain_list`. Email/Fireflies noise must not create
`core.customer` rows that PM/PIM, DAM, or PLM might mistake for real shared
customers.

Future sessions should:
When adding CRM ingestion behavior, call
`crm.record_ingested_domain(p_domain, p_sender, p_subject, p_display_name)` for
unknown non-noise domains. Only human promotion through
`crm.promote_ingested_domain(...)` should create a potential `core.customer`.
Do not switch the systemd units back to `crm-worker.mjs` unless deliberately
rolling back the Supabase CRM cutover.

### Workflow backfill is idempotent but already applied
What changed:

---

## POP CRM host workers

What changed:
POP CRM's host-side maintenance jobs now run the Supabase worker at
`pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs`. The installed units are:

- `popcrm-contact-sync.service`
- `popcrm-summarize.service`
- `popcrm-apply-ignore-rules.service`

Why:
CRM records now live in the shared Supabase backend instead of Directus
collections. The contact sync must record unknown domains in
`crm.ingested_domain` with `crm.record_ingested_domain(...)`; it must not insert
email-domain noise into `core.customer`.

Future sessions should:
Keep `/home/ai/.crm-worker.env` out of git. It must include `SUPABASE_URL` and
`SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` for the shared POP Supabase project. After changing
unit files, run `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` and verify with
`systemctl cat popcrm-contact-sync.service`. After changing the worker, run
`node --check pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` and a one-shot
`sudo systemctl start popcrm-contact-sync.service`.

arguments.notes:
Runtime env values for the POP CRM host-side Supabase worker on the Hetzner/Coolify host.

Host env file: /home/ai/.crm-worker.env
Repo code: /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs
Systemd unit templates: /worksp/popcrm-web/systemd/popcrm-*.service and .timer
Fireflies container: popcrm-fireflies bind-mounts /worksp/popcrm-web read-only and runs the same worker with fireflies-server.

Purpose:
These jobs talk to the shared Supabase backend directly. Contact sync records unknown domains through crm.record_ingested_domain(...) and must not create core.customer rows for email-domain noise.

Restore/check steps for future AI sessions:
1. Recreate /home/ai/.crm-worker.env with mode 600 and these SUPABASE_* values plus the existing Outlook/Fireflies/OpenRouter values from the host env as needed.
2. Run npm install in /worksp/popcrm-web if node_modules is absent; Node 20 needs the ws dependency used by workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs.
3. Install unit files from /worksp/popcrm-web/systemd/ to /etc/systemd/system/.
4. sudo systemctl daemon-reload
5. systemctl cat popcrm-contact-sync.service should point to /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs.
6. sudo systemctl start popcrm-contact-sync.service and verify it exits success.

/worksp/directus is legacy rollback/reference context only and must not be required by active POP CRM Supabase runtime.

Updated after the 2026-06-26 Directus decoupling session.

content.text:
{
  "id": "chdt363ktlbwd7n6wvksqqjqfq",
  "title": "DesignFlow PLM Canonical Master Data API",
  "category": "SecureNote",
  "vaultId": "b2dsir4jze3wfygdxixoaasdeq",
  "tags": [
    "designflow",
    "plm",
    "master-data",
    "api",
    "read-only",
    "supabase"
  ],
  "notes": "Purpose: Read-only DesignFlow PLM API used as the canonical source for Master Data matching/reference values.\n\nCanonical ownership:\n- Licensors: PLM is canonical.\n- Properties: PLM is canonical, returned with licensors from the licensor/property endpoint.\n- Customers: PLM is canonical.\n\nAuthentication:\n- Send the API key in the HTTP header `x-api-key`.\n- This key is read-only for these endpoints.\n- Do not put this key in browser/client-side code. Use it only from server-side scripts, migrations, edge functions, workers, or admin-only backend jobs.\n\nEndpoints:\n1. Licensors and properties\n   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties\n\n2. Customers\n   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers\n\nExample curl:\n  curl -sS \\\n    -H \"x-api-key: <read the api_key field from this 1Password item>\" \\\n    \"https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers\"\n\nHow this should be used in PopDAM / Master Data:\n- For Master Data / style tracker matching, use these APIs as the authoritative source instead of fuzzy matching against `core.company` when deciding customers.\n- For Licensor and Property dropdowns/matches, use the licensors/properties endpoint as the source of truth.\n- Existing shared tables such as `core.company` may contain imported CRM/Directus/Twenty data and may include non-customer accounts or noisy values. Treat those as secondary unless explicitly reconciled to PLM.\n- If syncing into Supabase, store source refs/provenance so future sessions know values came from PLM API, not Google Sheets or CRM.\n\nOriginal user-provided details, 2026-06-24:\n- Licensor & property API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties\n- Customers API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers\n- Accessible read-only with x-api-key stored in this item.",
  "sections": [
    {
      "id": "",
      "title": ""
    }
  ],
  "fields": [
    {
      "id": "api_key",
      "title": "api_key",
      "type": "Concealed",
      "section": "",
      "value": "[concealed]"
    },
    {
      "id": "auth_header",
      "title": "auth_header",
      "type": "Text",
      "section": "",
      "value": "x-api-key"
    },
    {
      "id": "licensors_properties_endpoint",
      "title": "licensors_properties_endpoint",
      "type": "Text",
      "section": "",
      "value": "https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties"
    },
    {
      "id": "customers_endpoint",
      "title": "customers_endpoint",
      "type": "Text",
      "section": "",
      "value": "https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers"
    },
    {
      "id": "scope",
      "title": "scope",
      "type": "Text",
      "section": "",
      "value": "read-only canonical PLM master data for licensors, properties, and customers"
    }
  ],
  "websites": [],
  "updatedAt": "2026-06-24T20:43:20.000Z"
}

payload.message: I found two more useful note updates: the shared Supabase key item should mention the new worker owner, and the PLM API item should point to the shared-db import tool. The Directus secret item can stay as Directus-only, but I’ll mark it legacy so it doesn’t look like shared-backend runtime.

The Directus secret item can stay as Directus-only, but I’ll mark it legacy so it doesn’t look like shared-backend runtime.

payload.arguments:
{"vaultId":"b2dsir4jze3wfygdxixoaasdeq","itemId":"sgk6fhcjdluqvnbsekjzdkftpa","notes":"Production Supabase runtime keys for the shared POP database used by CRM, PM/PIM, DAM, PLM, workers, and shared admin tooling.

Project ref: qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
Supabase URL: https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co

Use cases for future AI sessions:
- Rebuild server-side worker envs that need the service-role key.
- Verify PostgREST/API behavior through service-role probes.
- Compare app/runtime keys during Supabase cutover or restore work.

Current host-runtime ownership:
- POP CRM Supabase workers live in /worksp/popcrm-web/workers and use /home/ai/.crm-worker.env.
- PLM master-data import lives in /worksp/shared-db/tools and /worksp/shared-db/systemd.
- /worksp/directus is legacy rollback/reference context only for the current Supabase-based runtime.

Do not put service-role keys in browser app repos, GitHub Actions logs, docs, or .env files that are committed. Browser frontends should use only anon/publishable keys. Database migrations should usually use the separate item "Supabase DB Password - shared POP database" and /worksp/shared-db/AGENTS.md workflow.

Created after the 2026-06-26 CRM Supabase worker/contact-clear session because the production service-role/runtime keys were encountered but not stored under an obvious shared POP title.
Updated after the 2026-06-26 Directus decoupling session."}

arguments.notes:
Production Supabase runtime keys for the shared POP database used by CRM, PM/PIM, DAM, PLM, workers, and shared admin tooling.

Project ref: qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
Supabase URL: https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co

Use cases for future AI sessions:
- Rebuild server-side worker envs that need the service-role key.
- Verify PostgREST/API behavior through service-role probes.
- Compare app/runtime keys during Supabase cutover or restore work.

Current host-runtime ownership:
- POP CRM Supabase workers live in /worksp/popcrm-web/workers and use /home/ai/.crm-worker.env.
- PLM master-data import lives in /worksp/shared-db/tools and /worksp/shared-db/systemd.
- /worksp/directus is legacy rollback/reference context only for the current Supabase-based runtime.

Do not put service-role keys in browser app repos, GitHub Actions logs, docs, or .env files that are committed. Browser frontends should use only anon/publishable keys. Database migrations should usually use the separate item "Supabase DB Password - shared POP database" and /worksp/shared-db/AGENTS.md workflow.

Created after the 2026-06-26 CRM Supabase worker/contact-clear session because the production service-role/runtime keys were encountered but not stored under an obvious shared POP title.
Updated after the 2026-06-26 Directus decoupling session.

payload.name: item_edit
payload.arguments:
{"vaultId":"b2dsir4jze3wfygdxixoaasdeq","itemId":"chdt363ktlbwd7n6wvksqqjqfq","notes":"Purpose: Read-only DesignFlow PLM API used as the canonical source for Master Data matching/reference values.

Canonical ownership:
- Licensors: PLM is canonical.
- Properties: PLM is canonical, returned with licensors from the licensor/property endpoint.
- Customers: PLM is canonical.

Authentication:
- Send the API key in the HTTP header `x-api-key`.
- This key is read-only for these endpoints.
- Do not put this key in browser/client-side code. Use it only from server-side scripts, migrations, edge functions, workers, or admin-only backend jobs.

Endpoints:
1. Licensors and properties
   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties

2. Customers
   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers

Current Supabase import owner:
- Canonical repo: /worksp/shared-db
- Import tool: /worksp/shared-db/tools/sync-plm-master-data.mjs
- Host wrapper: /worksp/shared-db/tools/run-plm-master-data-sync.sh
- Host unit templates: /worksp/shared-db/systemd/plm-sync.service and .timer
- Host env file: /home/ai/.plm-sync.env

The active PLM master-data sync imports into the shared Supabase project through plm.import_master_data(...). It must not point to /worksp/directus or the old Directus Postgres container.

Example curl:
  curl -sS \
    -H "x-api-key: <read the api_key field from this 1Password item>" \
    "https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers"

How this should be used in PopDAM / Master Data:
- For Master Data / style tracker matching, use these APIs as the authoritative source instead of fuzzy matching against unverified app-local customer lists.
- For Licensor and Property dropdowns/matches, use the licensors/properties endpoint as the source of truth.
- If syncing into Supabase, store source refs/provenance so future sessions know values came from PLM API, not Google Sheets or CRM.

Original user-provided details, 2026-06-24:
- Licensor & property API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties
- Customers API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers
- Accessible read-only with x-api-key stored in this item.

Updated after the 2026-06-26 Directus decoupling session."}

payload.name: invoke_tool
payload.arguments:
{"name":"run_command","args":{"cwd":"/","timeout":120,"command":"set -euo pipefail
if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | rg -qx 'popcrm-fireflies'; then
  docker stop popcrm-fireflies >/dev/null || true
  docker rm popcrm-fireflies >/dev/null
fi
docker run -d \
  --name popcrm-fireflies \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  --network coolify \
  --env-file /home/ai/.crm-worker.env \
  -e PORT=8787 \
  -v /worksp/popcrm-web:/app:ro \
  -w /app \
  --label 'traefik.enable=true' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-http.entrypoints=http' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-http.middlewares=redirect-to-https@docker' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-http.rule=Host(`crm-fireflies.designflow.app`)' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-https.entrypoints=https' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-https.rule=Host(`crm-fireflies.designflow.app`)' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-https.tls=true' \
  --label 'traefik.http.routers.popcrm-fireflies-https.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt' \
  --label 'traefik.http.services.popcrm-fireflies.loadbalancer.server.port=8787' \
  node:20-alpine \
  node /app/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs fireflies-server
sleep 2
docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Command}}' | rg '^popcrm-fireflies\b'
docker logs --tail=20 popcrm-fireflies"}}

payload.name: invoke_tool

args.command:
set -euo pipefail
set -a
. /home/ai/.crm-worker.env
set +a
node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
const sb = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL, process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, { auth: { persistSession: false } })
async function count(schema, table) {
  const { count, error } = await sb.schema(schema).from(table).select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })
  if (error) throw error
  return count
}
console.log(JSON.stringify({ customers: await count('core', 'customer'), ingestedDomains: await count('crm', 'ingested_domain') }))
EOF

import WebSocket from 'ws'
const sb = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL, process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, { auth: { persistSession: false }, realtime: { transport: WebSocket } })
async function count(schema, table) {
  const { count, error } = await sb.schema(schema).from(table).select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })
  if (error) throw error
  return count
}
console.log(JSON.stringify({ customers: await count('core', 'customer'), ingestedDomains: await count('crm', 'ingested_domain') }))
EOF

}

payload.message: Post-count stayed `core.customer=3777`, so the contact sync did not create customer noise; ingested-domain count also stayed at 61, so the RPC behaved idempotently. I’ll run the ignore-rule sweep because it is a bounded one-shot; I’m going to skip summarize/reroute/outlook as live mutating/costly jobs outside this smoke test unless needed.

payload.message: One more important snag: the PLM wrapper uses `supabase db query --linked`, but this checkout has link metadata without a `supabase/config.toml`, so the CLI cannot find the project ref. I’m fixing the shared-db repo shape so its own documented linked-project path actually works.

content.text: One more important snag: the PLM wrapper uses `supabase db query --linked`, but this checkout has link metadata without a `supabase/config.toml`, so the CLI cannot find the project ref. I’m fixing the shared-db repo shape so its own documented linked-project path actually works.

payload.message: The failure is clear: systemd runs as `ai` and does not have the interactive Supabase CLI access token. I’m going to make the service use a database URL from its env file instead of relying on interactive CLI state; that’s the more durable host-worker pattern.

content.text: The failure is clear: systemd runs as `ai` and does not have the interactive Supabase CLI access token. I’m going to make the service use a database URL from its env file instead of relying on interactive CLI state; that’s the more durable host-worker pattern.

payload.message: I found the DB password item metadata. I’ll update `/home/ai/.plm-sync.env` in place using `op read` inside the shell so the password never appears in output, then rerun the PLM unit.

content.text: I found the DB password item metadata. I’ll update `/home/ai/.plm-sync.env` in place using `op read` inside the shell so the password never appears in output, then rerun the PLM unit.

payload.name: invoke_tool
payload.arguments:
{"name":"run_command","args":{"cwd":"/","timeout":120,"command":"set -euo pipefail
DB_PASS="$(op read 'op://vibe_coding/Supabase DB Password - shared POP database/password')"
POOLER_URL="$(cat /worksp/shared-db/supabase/.temp/pooler-url)"
SUPABASE_DB_URL="$(DB_PASS="$DB_PASS" POOLER_URL="$POOLER_URL" node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
const url = new URL(process.env.POOLER_URL)
url.password = process.env.DB_PASS
console.log(url.toString())
EOF
)"
if sudo grep -q '^SUPABASE_DB_URL=' /home/ai/.plm-sync.env; then
  TMP="$(mktemp)"
  sudo awk -v v="SUPABASE_DB_URL=$SUPABASE_DB_URL" 'BEGIN{done=0} /^SUPABASE_DB_URL=/{print v; done=1; next} {print} END{if(!done) print v}' /home/ai/.plm-sync.env > "$TMP"
  sudo install -m 600 -o ai -g ai "$TMP" /home/ai/.plm-sync.env
  rm -f "$TMP"
else
  printf '\nSUPABASE_DB_URL=%s\n' "$SUPABASE_DB_URL" | sudo tee -a /home/ai/.plm-sync.env >/dev/null
  sudo chown ai:ai /home/ai/.plm-sync.env
  sudo chmod 600 /home/ai/.plm-sync.env
fi
sudo awk -F= '/^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*=/{print $1}' /home/ai/.plm-sync.env | sort"}}

payload.name: item_edit
payload.arguments:
{"vaultId":"b2dsir4jze3wfygdxixoaasdeq","itemId":"chdt363ktlbwd7n6wvksqqjqfq","notes":"Purpose: Read-only DesignFlow PLM API used as the canonical source for Master Data matching/reference values.

Canonical ownership:
- Licensors: PLM is canonical.
- Properties: PLM is canonical, returned with licensors from the licensor/property endpoint.
- Customers: PLM is canonical.

Authentication:
- Send the API key in the HTTP header `x-api-key`.
- This key is read-only for these endpoints.
- Do not put this key in browser/client-side code. Use it only from server-side scripts, migrations, edge functions, workers, or admin-only backend jobs.

Endpoints:
1. Licensors and properties
   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties

2. Customers
   GET https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers

Current Supabase import owner:
- Canonical repo: /worksp/shared-db
- Import tool: /worksp/shared-db/tools/sync-plm-master-data.mjs
- Host wrapper: /worksp/shared-db/tools/run-plm-master-data-sync.sh
- Host unit templates: /worksp/shared-db/systemd/plm-sync.service and .timer
- Host env file: /home/ai/.plm-sync.env

Host env requirements for /home/ai/.plm-sync.env:
- PLM_API_KEY or DESIGNFLOW_API_KEY for the read-only PLM API.
- SUPABASE_DB_URL for the production Supabase pooler/database connection. This keeps systemd independent from an interactive Supabase CLI login.

The active PLM master-data sync imports into the shared Supabase project through plm.import_master_data(...). It must not point to /worksp/directus or the old Directus Postgres container.

Example curl:
  curl -sS \
    -H "x-api-key: <read the api_key field from this 1Password item>" \
    "https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers"

How this should be used in PopDAM / Master Data:
- For Master Data / style tracker matching, use these APIs as the authoritative source instead of fuzzy matching against unverified app-local customer lists.
- For Licensor and Property dropdowns/matches, use the licensors/properties endpoint as the source of truth.
- If syncing into Supabase, store source refs/provenance so future sessions know values came from PLM API, not Google Sheets or CRM.

Original user-provided details, 2026-06-24:
- Licensor & property API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master/lib/getLicensorsWithProperties
- Customers API endpoint: https://api.designflow.app/api/core/customers/getCustomers
- Accessible read-only with x-api-key stored in this item.

Updated after the 2026-06-26 Directus decoupling session."}

# CRM Backend Migration Plan: Directus To Shared Supabase

This document is the execution plan for migrating `u2giants/popcrm-web` from the
current Directus backend to the shared enterprise-wide Supabase backend.

It is written for a fresh AI/developer session with no prior chat context. Read
this file completely before making changes.

## Executive Summary

The CRM app currently reads and writes directly to a shared Directus API at
`https://data.designflow.app`. The new strategy is not to create a CRM-specific
Supabase project. Instead, CRM, PM/PIM, DAM, and operational PLM data will live
in one shared Supabase.com project with logical schemas.

The CRM migration has two parts:

1. Backend/data-layer work in the canonical shared database repo.
2. Frontend and worker rewrites in this CRM repo and the existing CRM worker.

Do not create a separate Supabase project for CRM.

Do not put permanent database DDL in this repo.

Do not run unreviewed SQL directly against production.

## Canonical Shared-DB References

The shared database plan is maintained in `u2giants/shared-db` and mirrored into
consumer repos under `shared-db/`. Mirrored copies are read-only and overwritten
by sync.

If the local checkout does not contain `shared-db/`, use the GitHub links below.

Primary shared-db docs:

- [Shared DB README](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/tree/main/shared-db)
- [Shared database vision](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/shared-database-vision.md)
- [Unified Supabase schema map](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md)

# POP CRM Frontend Beautification Implementation Plan

> **Status: historical plan — implemented.** This document is the original design
> brief and is kept for rationale (chart-library choice, design tokens, layout
> decisions). It describes the **pre-redesign** codebase, so file references like
> `src/features/crm/CrmPage.tsx` and `src/components/AppShell.tsx` no longer exist —
> they were replaced by `src/app/`, `src/features/crm/pages/`, and
> `src/components/app/`. For the **current** structure and conventions, read
> `AGENTS.md`. Do not treat this file as the current spec.

## Purpose

This document is the complete implementation brief for redesigning the POP CRM frontend into a polished, production-quality application using **Tailwind Plus Application UI** and a **token-themed chart library** (shadcn charts by default), while preserving the existing custom Directus-backed CRM logic.

It is written for a new developer with no prior context. If you are picking this up fresh, read this document first, then inspect the referenced files. The goal is to make the app beautiful, dense, credible, and operationally useful without replacing the working backend, data model, auth, migration, or custom CRM workflows.

## Executive Summary

The current CRM frontend works, but it is visually basic. It exposes many of the custom CRM functions that were migrated from Twenty into Directus, but it needs a professional application UI system: better shell/navigation, richer tables, better filters, better detail drawers, stronger hierarchy, polished empty/loading/error states, and analytics dashboards.

The user purchased **Tailwind Plus Application UI only**. Use it as the primary visual source for layouts and application patterns. Use a **token-themed chart library** (shadcn charts / Recharts by default — decided in Phase 0) for charts, KPI cards, trends, and data-rich dashboards. Keep the app as a custom Vite/React frontend.

Do **not** migrate the app to a new framework. Do **not** replace Directus SDK. Do **not** use the Directus Simple CRM template. Do **not** use ClickUp sync.

## Current Project

Repository:

- GitHub: `https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web`
- Local path: `/worksp/popcrm-web`
- Live production URL: `https://crm.designflow.app`
- Preview/dev alias: `https://crm-dev.designflow.app`
- Backend API: `https://data.designflow.app`
- Fireflies webhook/health: `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/health`

Current stack:

- Vite
- React 19
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS 4
- shadcn-style components built on Radix primitives
- Directus SDK
- Lucide icons
- Sonner toasts

Current important files:

- `src/App.tsx`: auth gate and app shell wrapper.
- `src/auth/auth.tsx`: Directus auth state.
- `src/components/AppShell.tsx`: current minimal authenticated shell.
- `src/components/ui/*`: existing shadcn-style primitives.
- `src/features/crm/CrmPage.tsx`: current monolithic CRM workbench.
- `src/features/crm/api.ts`: Directus SDK reads/writes.
- `src/lib/types.ts`: Directus collection types used by the frontend.
- `src/lib/directus.ts`: Directus client config.
- `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`: login screen.

Current deployed app state:

- The app serves `POP CRM`.
- `crm.designflow.app` points to `popcrm-web`, not POPPIM.
- Login branding was fixed from `POP PIM` to `POP CRM`.
- The app currently loads full CRM lists for core records.

## Current Backend and Data Context

The CRM is not based on Directus's Marketplace Simple CRM template. It is custom.

Backend repo:

- GitHub: `https://github.com/u2giants/directus`
- Local path: `/worksp/directus`

Relevant backend files:

- `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-schema.mjs`: custom Directus CRM collections/fields/relations.
- `/worksp/directus/pm-system/migration/twenty-import.mjs`: migration from Twenty into Directus.
- `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs`: Outlook ingest, rerouting, Fireflies webhook, contact sync, opportunity summaries.
- `/worksp/directus/pm-system/systemd/*`: systemd timers/services for worker tasks.

Important backend facts:

- ClickUp sync is intentionally omitted.
- Outlook ingest runs every 15 minutes.
- Reroute runs every 6 hours.
- Contact sync runs daily.
- Opportunity summary refresh runs every 6 hours.
- Fireflies webhook is live and routes meeting notes into CRM.

+## POP PIM / Supabase inventory
+
+`poppim-web` is the PIM frontend for POP Creations and is live at `https://pm.designflow.app`; aliases `pm-dev.designflow.app` and `pm-ci.designflow.app` point at the same Coolify service. It is a static React/Vite SPA with no local database. It reads/writes the shared Supabase.com backend.
+
+| Component | Runtime owner | Identifier |
+|---|---|---|
+| PIM frontend | Coolify service `poppim-web` | UUID `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`, project `jdq36h5dq74o6ddhich9l796` |
+| PIM image | GHCR | `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web` |
+| Backend | Supabase.com | Project `qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai`, URL `https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co` |
+| Source | GitHub | `u2giants/poppim-web`; shared backend schema in `u2giants/shared-db` |
+
+Deployment is `git push main` -> GitHub Actions -> GHCR -> Coolify. For this service, the workflow patches the service compose to an immutable `:sha-<commit>` image and then restarts it. Do not deploy PIM with raw `docker run`, server file edits, or `/api/v1/deploy?uuid=`.
+
+## Application inventory: POP CRM
+
+`popcrm-web` is the POP Creations CRM frontend.
+
+| Item | Value |
+|------|-------|
+| Production URL | `https://crm.designflow.app` |
+| Preview alias | `https://crm-dev.designflow.app` |
+| Coolify app | `popcrm-web` (`a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt`) |
+| Coolify project | POP Creations CRM (`yp84tp0tmmshhcebgsd4j463`) |
+| Coolify server | localhost (`onwp0kd7w1w74w9yeotnoihp`) |
+| Image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web` |
+| Deploy path | `main` push -> GitHub Actions -> GHCR -> Coolify deploy API |
+| Backend | Supabase project `qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai` (`https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`) |
+| Related worker | `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app` (`popcrm-fireflies`, code owned by `/worksp/popcrm-web/workers`) |
+
+CRM app config is build-time Vite config, not runtime container env:
+`VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, and optional publishable
+`VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` are baked into the static bundle during the Docker build.
+Never bake service-role keys or backend secrets into the frontend image.
+

+dependencies, or incident runbooks change, update both this infrastructure

payload.message:
## Directus Decoupling Result

- `/worksp/directus` can be deleted without affecting current Supabase CRM runtime: **yes**
- Deletion/rename simulation tested: **yes**. Temporarily moved `/worksp/directus` to `/worksp/directus.DELETE_ME_TEST`; Fireflies health, CRM contact sync, ignore-rule sweep, PLM sync, process checks, systemd checks, and container mount checks all passed.
- Remaining blocker: **none for runtime**. Note: `albert-standards` has pre-existing unrelated dirty/ahead/behind state, so I left its repo uncommitted and called it out below.

## Moved Runtime Ownership

| Old path | New path | Runtime caller |
|---|---|---|
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` | `/worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` | `popcrm-*` systemd services, `popcrm-fireflies` |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs` for Outlook/reroute | `/worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` | `popcrm-outlook-ingest.service`, `popcrm-reroute.service` |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/run-plm-sync.sh` | `/worksp/shared-db/tools/run-plm-master-data-sync.sh` | `plm-sync.service` |

## Live Host Changes

| Unit/timer/process | New ExecStart / path | Verified |
|---|---|---|
| `popcrm-outlook-ingest.service` | `/usr/bin/node /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs outlook-ingest` | yes |
| `popcrm-reroute.service` | `/usr/bin/node /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs reroute` | yes |
| `popcrm-contact-sync.service` | `/usr/bin/node /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs contact-sync` | yes |
| `popcrm-summarize.service` | `/usr/bin/node /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs summarize` | yes |
| `popcrm-apply-ignore-rules.service` | `/usr/bin/node /worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs apply-ignore-rules` | yes |
| `plm-sync.service` | `/usr/bin/bash /worksp/shared-db/tools/run-plm-master-data-sync.sh` | yes |
| `popcrm-fireflies` | bind mount `/worksp/popcrm-web:/app:ro`, command `/app/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs fireflies-server` | yes |

## 1Password Updates

| Item | Change |
|---|---|
| `POP CRM Supabase Worker Env - hetz /home/ai/.crm-worker.env` | Notes + `WORKER_FILE` now point to `/worksp/popcrm-web/workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` |
| `Supabase Runtime Keys - shared POP database (production)` | Notes now document worker ownership in `popcrm-web` and PLM import ownership in `shared-db` |
| `DesignFlow PLM Canonical Master Data API` | Notes now document `/worksp/shared-db` PLM sync and `SUPABASE_DB_URL` requirement |
| `directus-secrets` | Marked Directus-only legacy; not used by current Supabase CRM/PLM runtime |

## Verification

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `node --check workers/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` | pass |
| `npm ls @supabase/supabase-js ws --depth=0` | pass |
| `npm run lint` / `npm run build` in `popcrm-web` | pass |
| `node --check tools/sync-plm-master-data.mjs` / `bash -n tools/run-plm-master-data-sync.sh` | pass |
| `popcrm-contact-sync.service` | success; `core.customer` stayed `3777`, `crm.ingested_domain` stayed `61` |
| `popcrm-apply-ignore-rules.service` | success; `0 emails skipped by 0 rules` |
| `plm-sync.service` | success; imported `37` licensors, `468` properties, `55` customers, `560` raw records |
| Fireflies health | `{"ok":true}` |
| `/worksp/directus` rename simulation | pass |
| Installed systemd refs to `/worksp/directus` | none |
| Running process refs to `/worksp/directus` | none |

## Git

| Repo | Commit/PR | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `/worksp/popcrm-web` | `ce8b4e7` pushed to `main` | clean |
| `/worksp/directus` | `047e8b5` pushed to `main` | clean |
| `/worksp/shared-db` | PR [#18](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/pull/18), merged as `4b94c72` | clean |
| `/worksp/albert-standards` | not committed | pre-existing dirty state remains; includes unrelated modified/untracked files and branch is ahead 1/behind 25 |

::git-stage{cwd="/worksp/popcrm-web"}
::git-commit{cwd="/worksp/popcrm-web"}
::git-push{cwd="/worksp/popcrm-web" branch="main"}
::git-stage{cwd="/worksp/directus"}
::git-commit{cwd="/worksp/directus"}
::git-push{cwd="/worksp/directus" branch="main"}
::git-create-branch{cwd="/worksp/shared-db" branch="codex/directus-decoupling-plm-workers"}
::git-stage{cwd="/worksp/shared-db"}
::git-commit{cwd="/worksp/shared-db"}
::git-push{cwd="/worksp/shared-db" branch="codex/directus-decoupling-plm-workers"}
::git-create-pr{cwd="/worksp/shared-db" branch="codex/directus-decoupling-plm-workers" url="https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/pull/18" isDraft=false}
